items.title: Linux epoll bug enables root escalation
items.oneliner: Bad Epoll lets unprivileged code reach root, even from Chrome's sandbox, making Linux and Android patch urgency very real.

items.title: Claude tool calling appears to regress
items.oneliner: Newer Claude models look smarter but break more tool calls, which matters more than benchmark gains in production agents.

items.title: GPT-5.5 Codex may be budget clipping
items.oneliner: Exact reasoning-token spikes at 516, 1034, and 1552 suggest GPT-5.5 Codex may be silently capping hard tasks.

items.title: Claude built a release for $149
items.oneliner: Simon Willison says Claude Fable wrote most of sqlite-utils 4.0rc2 for $149.25, shrinking the cost of solo shipping.

items.title: AI watermarking claims look overstated
items.oneliner: Independent tests say SynthID and TrustMark miss badly, so provenance layers still look too weak for compliance workflows.

items.title: Event logs are becoming the agent
items.oneliner: ActiveGraph makes the append-only log the system of record, enabling replay, lineage, and cheap forks for agent debugging.

items.title: Tailscale is prototyping universal undo
items.oneliner: Thundersnap treats snapshots like an undo stack for systems, hinting at safer experimentation across containers, filesystems, and hosts.

items.title: shadcn/ui switches its default foundation
items.oneliner: shadcn/ui now defaults to Base UI over Radix, a meaningful ecosystem shift for teams standardizing React components.

items.title: AI infrastructure costs are hitting reality
items.oneliner: A trillion-dollar buildout is colliding with power, water, and investor limits, so AI scale now looks like an infrastructure problem.

items.title: Immich ships a major self-hosted update
items.oneliner: Immich 3.0 adds non-destructive mobile editing and real-time video transcoding, raising the bar for self-hosted photo stacks.
